content_of_post_to_comment: Age discrimination is one of the strangest things we do to each other. When you’re young, people tell you that you don’t know enough. When you’re older, they tell you that you’ve had your time. So when exactly are people allowed to exist without apology? Youth is not a crime. Age is not a failure. Growing older is not something to be ashamed of. In fact, it’s a privilege many people never get. Wrinkles are not evidence that life is over. Gray hair is not proof that dreams expire. And experience is not something to be hidden. Funny enough, we admire old trees, old books, old wine, and old souls. But somehow we’ve convinced people to fear becoming what they are lucky enough to become. There is nothing embarrassing about aging. The embarrassing part is a society that treats growing older like a tragedy instead of a gift.
